pilot_scope: ND-only pilot based on reqret-001; other cases deferred until the first case is reviewed.

llm_judge_contract.judge_model_policy: Use one strong judge model for v1. Keep report metadata compatible with future multi-judge or arbiter setups.

anchors.1: weak; mentions the right area but misses the substantive requirement
anchors.2: partial; captures some correct meaning but misses important nuance or evidence support
anchors.3: good; materially correct, grounded, and useful
anchors.4: excellent; complete, precise, well-grounded, and appropriately caveated

llm_judge_contract.numeric_confidence_policy: Judge parser accepts numeric confidence in the 0-1 range and normalizes it to low, medium, or high while preserving confidence_numeric.
llm_judge_contract.per_article_review_normalization_policy: Judge parser accepts compact per-article review fields from live judges and normalizes notes/relevance_label_correct/must_cover_points_hit/forbidden_claim_violations into the canonical review fields.

candidate_statuses.judgeable: Candidate output parsed against its benchmark contract and can receive semantic judge scores.
candidate_statuses.candidate_parse_error: Candidate output failed JSON parsing or required top-level shape before semantic judging.
candidate_statuses.candidate_schema_error_judgeable: Candidate output has a limited schema/rubric issue but enough structured content remains for semantic judging.
candidate_statuses.candidate_schema_error_blocked: Candidate output is too malformed for semantic judging and should not receive semantic scores.

llm_judge_contract.analogue_tolerance_policy: For article-level synthesis candidate outputs, thesis.supports=analogue can be treated as candidate_schema_error_judgeable for LLM judge review; this does not change the formal benchmark output schema.
llm_judge_contract.storage_policy: Raw judge outputs and full reports stay under ignored benchmark/results/; compact QA notes may be committed when needed for traceability.

output_contract.article_coverage: The model must return exactly one article_summaries item for every input article.
output_contract.irrelevant_policy: Irrelevant and distractor articles must be labeled explicitly and must not be converted into substantive supporting theses.
output_contract.request_specificity: Summaries and theses must explain what the article contributes to the Avito New Developments request, not provide a generic article recap.
output_contract.full_text_usage: The model may use body_full_text and body_excerpt, but should cite only article_id evidence, not quote long passages.

article_summaries.request_specific_summary: The article is an analogue for using first-party buyer behavior to prioritize follow-up, but it does not directly validate developer CPA pricing.

theses.statement: Behavioral intent signals can help prioritize sales follow-up.
theses.evidence: The article describes activity signals being used to decide which lead needs immediate follow-up.

article_summaries.avito_implication: Useful as an analogue for Avito ND lead scoring, with caution that the monetization model is not direct developer CPA proof.
caveats: Adjacent residential workflow evidence, not a direct new-development portal pricing case.

input_selection_profile.source: Copied from request-synthesis ND pilot input to keep whole-set and per-article tasks comparable.
input_selection_profile.notes: The set intentionally includes direct new-homes/lead-scoring evidence, adjacent CRM/property-data analogues, risk articles, and one generic AI marketing distractor.

deterministic_v1.purpose: Baseline scoring for schema-valid model outputs before semantic judge calibration.

metrics.article_relevance_accuracy: Exact-match accuracy between predicted and golden relevance labels per article.
metrics.must_point_recall: Share of golden must-cover points matched by deterministic keyword/phrase cues where configured; this is a proxy, not full semantic recall.
metrics.grounding: Checks that every article summary refers only to the matching input article and uses valid thesis/evidence fields.
metrics.overstatement_penalty: Penalizes exact or near-exact forbidden claims from must_not_claim and high relevance assigned to low/distractor articles.
metrics.distractor_handling: Distractor articles must be labeled irrelevant or low and should not contain substantive confirming theses.
metrics.implication_quality: For high/medium articles, avito_implication must be non-empty and must not contradict must_not_claim guards.

pass_policy.article_coverage: all input articles must be present exactly once
pass_policy.forbidden_claims: no high-severity forbidden or must_not_claim violation

known_limits: Deterministic must-point matching can over-credit keyword overlap and under-credit valid paraphrase.
Semantic quality and expert correctness still require human or LLM judge review.
The scorer should report its heuristic status in result artifacts.

semantic_llm_judge_v2.purpose: Evaluate semantic coverage, grounding quality, request specificity, and nuanced analogue-vs-proof distinctions after deterministic v1 is calibrated.

pilot_run.observed_issue: Two models used thesis.supports=analogue, which is outside the AS1 enum; AS7 should decide whether to allow analogue here or keep the stricter supports enum.
